summary.intent: Investigate and remediate failures in the latest Nightly CI run for the ars-ui repository, fixing a pagination proptest failure and multiple mutation testing survivor clusters across several components.
summary.outcome: Fixed the pagination controlled-page bounds invariant (controlled Bindable not clamping when page_count shrinks), added mutation-killing regression tests across 9 components, updated the Nightly workflow to enable the i18n feature during ars-components mutation runs (closing a systematic blind spot), updated the pagination spec, and committed/pushed a PR after passing all local gates including cargo xci-fast.

repo: The utility/highlight module is gated behind the ars-components/i18n feature flag; the Nightly mutation workflow was running cargo mutants without that feature, making all highlight mutations unobservable — mutations showed as survivors but were never actually compiled.
cargo xci-fast is the required 10-gate fast-profile check that must pass before committing; it gates fmt, check, clippy, tests, spec validation, and more.
The repo requires invoking .agents/skills/post-implementation-audit/SKILL.md before committing and .agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md after each push.
Mutation jobs use --shard N/M across 12 parallel GitHub Actions jobs for ars-components; each shard uploads its own missed.txt artifact on failure.

Controlled Bindable values in pagination return the caller-supplied value even after an internal set(), so page_count shrinking via SetPageSize could expose current_page() > page_count() without explicit mirror synchronization.

Equivalent mutants (e.g., provisional new_x/new_y assignments overwritten before return in resize_rect, or mailto:/tel: both mapping to the same policy) require narrow line-scoped exclusions in .cargo/mutants.toml with documented justifications — bulk exclusions are rejected by convention.

spec/components/navigation/pagination.md is the source-of-truth spec that must be updated when a public contract is clarified; adapter specs (spec/leptos-components, spec/dioxus-components) inherit from it.

code.finding: SetPageSize must explicitly clamp and sync the controlled-page mirror when the new page_count is smaller than the currently-controlled page; without this, ctx.page.get() returns the stale controlled value and violates the 1 <= page <= page_count invariant.
Zero-length chunk edge case (empty match at boundary) was untested; without a test asserting chunk count for a zero-length match, the boundary condition mutant survived.

Provisional new_x/new_y assignments for west/north corner handles are immediately overwritten by right/bottom edge recomputation, making those initial assignments equivalent mutants; narrowly excluded by source line in mutants.toml.
mailto: and tel: URL schemes intentionally map to the same policy branch, making the discriminating condition an equivalent mutant; documented narrowly in mutants.toml.
ars-components mutation shards were missing --features ars-components/i18n, causing all highlight mutations (and any other i18n-gated code) to be skipped as unviable rather than tested.

Timeout-only loop mutants in download_trigger's percent-decoder loop require cargo-mutants exclude syntax matching the -=/+= operator forms, not raw arithmetic; the regex must match the mutated operator string as cargo-mutants reports it.

workflow: Always trigger a fresh CI run on current HEAD before acting on stale failure artifacts; in this session the failed run was on commit 98a7d5c but main had advanced 4 commits to cd6760c before investigation began.
Use cargo mutants --shard N/M to split large files into parallel local reruns and avoid session-limit interruptions on files with hundreds of mutants.
Use cargo mutants -p <pkg> -f <file> for focused per-file mutation reruns after adding tests; full-package reruns are slow and unnecessary when validating a single survivor cluster.
GitHub Actions job logs return BlobNotFound until the job completes; artifact downloads are the reliable way to get mutation results, not live log streaming.
When a mutation run is started against a file that has since been edited (tests added mid-run), the in-flight results reflect the old snapshot — always rerun after completing edits to the file.
Equivalent mutant exclusions must use narrow function/line scope in mutants.toml, not file-level or module-level exclusions, to avoid masking future regressions in the same file.
cargo llvm-cov test --lib is a useful final check to confirm that new regression tests actually exercise the changed code paths before committing.

friction: The failed Nightly run was on a stale commit; main had advanced 4 commits before investigation started, requiring a fresh manual workflow dispatch and a full re-download of mutation artifacts before any fix could be validated against current HEAD.
GitHub Actions returns BlobNotFound for job logs while jobs are still running, forcing polling via the jobs API and waiting for artifact uploads rather than streaming live output.

The highlight mutation blind spot (missing --features ars-components/i18n in the workflow) was not obvious from the artifact filenames alone; it surfaced only after reading the Cargo.toml feature gates and correlating them with the cargo mutants command in the workflow.
Large cargo-mutants runs (floating_panel ~3000+ mutants) were interrupted by session limits, requiring manual sharding with --shard 0/4 through 3/4 as a workaround.

Multiple mutation reruns were needed for download_trigger because the exclude regex syntax for timeout-only loop counter mutants had to be tuned to match cargo-mutants' exact operator wording (-=, +=) across two iterations.
The 12 ars-components mutation shards on GitHub ran for over an hour, and failed shards did not all complete simultaneously — artifacts had to be downloaded in multiple batches as jobs finished.

A test assertion bug (using the default close_on_escape value instead of explicitly setting it to false) caused a false baseline failure in floating_panel tests, requiring a correction pass before the mutation rerun could proceed.
open_items: The PR needs to receive a green Nightly run on the PR branch and Codex review approval before merge; waiting-for-codex-review was invoked but outcome is pending.

Only 7 of the 12 failed component mutation shards from the fresh run were explicitly triaged (shards 2, 3, 5, 8, 10, 11, 12); the overlap between shard boundaries and the files touched means some survivor clusters from shards not downloaded may reappear on the next Nightly if they map to untouched files.

The hover_card idempotent title-registration mutant fix was added but hover_card was not in the original fresh shard-11 artifact — it was inferred from the stale run; this should be confirmed on the next Nightly run.

cargo llvm-cov showed 97.39% line coverage for ars-components with i18n, but branch/region coverage was not checked; the new controlled-page clamp path has multiple branches that warrant a targeted branch-coverage pass.

The post-implementation audit noted that adapter specs (leptos, dioxus) were not updated with the pagination controlled-page clamping invariant — only the core spec was updated; adapter specs should be reviewed for alignment.
